Staff Parking Available. * 5 minute walk to Broombridge station (Dublin\-Sligo line) and Phisboro Luas Station (Luas Green Line) **Role Description** This is a full\-time on\-site role for a Legal Intern at Doyle \& Company LLP. Handling phone calls, clerical tasks, communicating with clients, and providing excellent customer service. The role will also assist in the running of the legal cases firm and receive on\-job training in various aspects of a general practice legal firm. **Qualifications** * Suitable to a graduate interested in pursuing a professional qualification in law and looking to gain experience in a legal firm and build a carear. * Phone Etiquette and Communication skills. * Customer Service skills. * Strong organizational and multitasking abilities. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. * Attention to detail and problem\-solving skills. * Legal studies background or internship experience is a plus.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Level 1 IT Support Analyst
description: We are seeking a proactive Desktop Support Analyst, with experience in customer\-facing environments, to join our IT support team. This role is critical in ensuring end users receive a high standard of technical assistance across hardware, software, and enterprise applications. You will play a key role in maintaining business productivity by resolving incidents efficiently and delivering an exceptional user experience. Key Responsibilities * Diagnose and resolve issues with desktops, laptops, and otther office/IT devices. * Troubleshoot issues operating systems (Windows 11\), Microsoft 365 applications, and other software issues and errors. * Manage incidents and requests through a ticketing platform. * Manage accounts across Active Directory, email platforms, etc. * Install, configure, and update software. * Support users by addressing and resolving issues using remote management tools. * Prepare new equipment including imaging, asset tagging, and software deployment. * Ensure all devices are maintained to a high standard and that all antivirus, encryption, and software is up to date. What you'll bring * Minimum 2 years' IT and technical supoport experience, ideally with Microsoft 365 services including Teams and OneDrive. * Hands\-on experience with ITSM ticketing systems \- essential. * Knowledge of mobile device support and Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions. * Strong background in desktop hardware troubleshooting and end\-user support. Desirable skills \& capabilities * Very good communication skills. * Ability to maintain documentation to a good and comprehensive standard. * A "customer first" approach. * Strong time and workload management skill. * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills. If you are a driven Desktop Support professional looking for an opportunity to make a tangible impact within a dynamic organisation, we would love to hear from you. Apply today.

title: Maintenance Assistant
description: You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty. * 50% Off F\&B in all properties. * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels. * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate. * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days). * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes. * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information). * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey. * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events. **Your Job** * Manage daily workload effectively, utilizing resources for painting, decorating, and gardening. * Perform maintenance tasks, including preventative routines in tiling, plumbing, and carpentry. * Investigate and resolve equipment and control system issues. * Assist plumbers and electricians with defects in hotel systems. * Collaborate with all hotel departments. * Ensure maintenance work meets professional and safety standards. * Comply with health and safety regulations and HACCP procedures. **What You Will Need** * Previous hospitality experience is preferred. * Safe Pass certification is a plus. * Qualifications in trades such as painting, plumbing, or construction are desirable. * Self\-motivated and able to prioritize tasks. * Detail\-oriented and standards\-driven. * Ability to multitask in a fast\-paced environment. * Flexibility in working hours is required. * Good command of English **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group Plc is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. title: Marketing Internship 2026.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. **Location/Office Policy:** 5 days a week, with 3 in\-person in our office in Central Park, Leopardstown, Dublin. **Duration:** This is a 12 month internship * Do you want to take the first step towards building a career in Marketing, with a particular focus on sponsorship? * Are you obsessed with designing amazing customer experiences? * Do you love seeing something come to life that you were involved with right from the brief? **What Is The Role** **Working on a dynamic and fast paced team in Group Marketing, the candidate will be required to:** * Work with partners, agencies, rights\-holders and colleagues to provide world class sponsorship and experiences for our customers * Take responsibility for completing tasks and contribute to problem solving * Have a passion for marketing, particularly sponsorship, and be dedicated to building world\-class experiences that will amaze our customers We’re one of Ireland’s biggest banks, and we have a passionate marketing team who are customer obsessed. We are a function that is made of small teams where you have the chance to shine, whether it is understanding customers insights, developing experiences that wow our customers or ensuring we are reaching our customers in the right way. In each team you’ll find leaders who focus as much on people as they do on function. Here, anyone who is responsible for managing people is called a people leader, not a manager. That shows our commitment and focus on the development and engagement of the people who work here. You’ll find a mix of age, race and gender, where everyone has an opportunity to reach their potential. You’ll find a culture of openness, positivity and respect where everyone has a voice. We are looking for the best experienced people as well as graduates. We will match your energy and enthusiasm with empowerment so that together we can build a culture that breaks the conventions of what our customer and employees expect of a bank. **Key Accountabilities** * Own the end‑to‑end coordination and delivery of assigned sponsorship and experiential activities * Maintain, and manage controls on team budgets with PO responsibility * Act as a central point of liaison across internal stakeholders and external partners * Proactively update internal registers with group\-wide reach to ensure they are correct * Coordinate operational elements in support of projects e.g. event logistics, stakeholder communications, content drafts, and asset management * Support the broader team as needed with lots of potential for on\-the\-job learning beyond the role **What You Will Bring** * Initiative and accountability in executing the tasks set to the highest standards * Effective communication skills which can flex to interact with colleagues, stakeholders and partners * Be a strong team player and understand the importance of working with others * Focus on measurement and effectiveness **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. **Some Of Our Benefits Include** * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Customer Focus – Keeping customer front of mind and developing customer centric solutions * Collaborates \- Building partnerships and working collaboratively with others to meet shared objectives. * Ensures Accountability \- Holding self and others accountable to meet commitments. If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the recruiter for this role, Sophie Doherty, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Disclaimer** Unsolicited CV’s sent to AIB by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this position. AIB operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. **Application deadline : Friday 29th of May**

title: Material Controller
description: **The Opportunity** Avantor is looking for a Material Controller to join our team in Dunboyne on a full\-time basis. You will be based in a laboratory related environment, working alongside your team colleagues, and supporting scientific processes to meet customer business needs. This opportunity is ideal for someone coming from retail, warehouse, hospitality environment, who would like to join a business leading company with possibilities of progression. We are open to applicants from all industries, as training will be provided. **The team** You are part of a dedicated team of associates and able to work closely together, you will be part of a team of 3 with a phenomenal group atmosphere. **What we’re looking for** * Education: High school completion or equivalent (GED); Bachelor’s degree preferred * Experience: Longevity and consistent work history is desirable. This can be general warehouse, hospitality, Inventory, accounting or retail. * Additional experience: Friendly and hard working with great positive attitude, general warehousing terminology and practices \- we are open to considering all backgrounds! * Collaboration Tool: Good IT skills required (including Microsoft Word/Excel/Outlook) preferred **Who You Are** * You thrive off a challenge and are not afraid to get really stuck in * Have a driving license and own vehicle, and can commute easily to Meath * Comfortable working in customer\-centric environments **How you will thrive and create an impact** * Machine \& Hand wash and perform a quality check and inspection of all glassware for damages and cleanliness, ensure disposal of damaged glassware, record all details accurately * Stock up all glassware in regulated laboratories, ensuring we have the right stock levels and ordering stock in where required, including adhoc order requests * Cleaning and regular decontamination of a variety of specialist laboratory equipment * Operate all equipment in accordance with procedural documents, site, and regulatory requirements. * Maintain and update records for equipment servicing and routine experiments, along with any other relevant administrative tasks **Disclaimer** The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this classification. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required of employees assigned to this position. Avantor is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. **Why Avantor?** **Dare to go further in your career.** Join our global team of 14,000\+ associates whose passion for discovery and determination to overcome challenges relentlessly advances life\-changing science. **The work we do changes people's lives for the better.** It brings new patient treatments and therapies to market, giving a cancer survivor the chance to walk his daughter down the aisle. It enables medical devices that help a little boy hear his mom's voice for the first time. Outcomes such as these create unlimited opportunities for you to contribute your talents, learn new skills and grow your career at Avantor. We are committed to helping you on this journey through our diverse, equitable and inclusive culture which includes learning experiences to support your career growth and success. At Avantor, dare to go further and see how the impact of your contributions set science in motion to create a better world. **Apply today!** **EEO Statement** We are an Equal Employment/Affirmative Action employer and VEVRAA Federal Contractor. We do not discriminate in hiring on the basis of sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religious creed, national origin, physical or mental disability, protected Veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state/province, or local law. Requests for accommodation will be considered on a case\-by\-case basis. Please note that only inquiries concerning a request for reasonable accommodation will be responded to from this email address. **Privacy Policy** We will use the personal information that you have submitted to us in order to consider your application for the relevant role. Your privacy is important to us. Please click here for our Privacy Policy which explains the purposes for which we will use your personal information and the ways in which we will handle and retain your information. title: Maths, Science and AEN/SEN
company: Kilkenny And Carlow Education And Training Board
description: **Teaching and learning is the core focus of Kilkenny and Carlow ETB and we are committed to recruiting high quality teachers with appropriate qualifications, expertise and experience.** **Qualifications** ***It is an advantage if the candidate has a post graduate qualification in Special Education Needs/Learning Support or be in the process of applying for or completing this qualification.*** Applicants must meet the general and special requirements as set out from time to time by the Teaching Council of Ireland for the advertised subject(s). Further details and a full list of recognised qualifications are available on www.teachingcouncil.ie. Applicants who are in their final year of degree/postgraduate diploma which will lead to a teaching qualification, but who have not yet completed their final exams and/or received their results, may apply for advertised vacancies, subject to the provisions regarding Teaching Council registration set out below. Any offer of interview or employment is made pending the applicant’s results meeting the requirements of the vacancy. **Registration with the Teaching Council of Ireland (Essential)** Applicants must be currently registered with the Teaching Council of Ireland. Applicants shall provide a current Registration Certificate, or confirmation of Registration with their Application Form for a teaching position. **Teaching****Competences** Persons appointed to teaching positions within Kilkenny and Carlow ETB will be required to show evidence of the following key competences: * + Professional Knowledge + Professional Practice + Communication and relationship building + Self\-awareness and self\-management skills Candidates will be required to describe in no more than 300 words per competency some of their personal achievements to\-date that demonstrate the key competencies required for the position as outlined. Candidates should describe situation/s from their own experience, which they think are the best example/s of what they have done which demonstrates the specific competencies. The example(s) may be drawn from candidates experience in various settings including their professional, community or voluntary involvement. **Professional Knowledge** *Demonstrate in\-depth knowledge, comprehension and understanding of both the subject discipline and pedagogy* *Indicators include:* * Has a strong knowledge of subject matter. * Has an awareness and understanding of specific requirements of course/syllabus/specifications. * Keeps up to date with subject matter/course syllabus changes and developments in the education system. * Understands the cross\-curricular links to other content areas and is able to integrate learning across and between content areas (e.g. literacy, numeracy, critical thinking). * Knows, understands and critically evaluates the range of teaching and learning philosophies / theories and knows how to apply them appropriately within their subject (s). * Is aware of the range of supports available to support curriculum development and implementation. * Participates in and contributes to educational planning and development within the school e.g. lesson planning, subject department planning, etc. **Professional Practice** *Understands the nature of high quality teaching and learning and demonstrates the skills and competencies to illustrate these.* *Indicators include:* ***Planning and Preparation*** * Plans and communicates clear, challenging, appropriate and achievable expectations for students. * Shows knowledge and understanding of how to structure and schedule schemes of work, class plans, subject department plans etc. * Knows and understands the relationship between the planning, implementation and evaluation of the curriculum. * Selects, creates and pitches class materials which respond to the needs of students. **Teaching and Learning** * Understands the factors that contribute to a stimulating learning environment and the role of the teacher in supporting this environment. * Understands the practices that underpin effective classroom teaching and meet the learning styles of a diverse range of students e.g. differentiation, learning intentions, group work, etc. * Establishes standards of behaviour that enable all students to learn and deals with inappropriate behaviour in the context of school policies. * Demonstrates in\-depth knowledge and understanding of classroom management techniques and skills. * Effectively manages the range of behaviours/situations that can occur in the classroom in line with School Code of Behaviour maintaining good order and discipline among students. * Effectively uses a variety of teaching materials and technologies to actively engage students in the learning process e.g. collaboration, technology enhanced teaching and learning. ***Assessment*** * Focuses on assessment for learning by monitoring student progress, giving constructive feedback and helping students to reflect on and improve their learning e.g. formative and summative assessment. * Selects from a range of assessment strategies to evaluate student learning, and uses this information in their planning to help make their teaching more effective. * Assesses the levels of student attainment against relevant benchmarking data and understands the relationship between student assessment and target setting. * Liaises orally and in written reports in an effective manner with parents/guardians on student progress and achievement. **Communication and relationship building** *Establishes effective communication and engagement to ensure positive and effective working relationships with management, colleagues, students, parents and the wider school community.* *Indicators include:* * Seeks to foster positive relationships with students, colleagues, parents, school management and the wider school community in a professional, collaborative, supportive and respectful manner. * Communicates and contributes effectively and collaboratively within the school team environment. * Shows the capacity and skills to relate to and communicate effectively with students, parents, colleagues, school management and the wider school community. * Supports conflict resolution in all aspects of teaching role. * Motivates, inspires and celebrates student effort and success. * Is committed to equality and inclusion and to respecting and accommodating diversity. * Understands how to exercise a duty of care to students. * Participates in and supports the extra\-curricular programmes/activities of the school. * Contributes positively to the school community. **Self\-awareness and self\-management skills** *Is self\-aware and has the capacity to self\-manage and develop personally and professionally*. *Indicators include:* * Engages in reflective practice of their own work and consistently reviews it in order to ensure it is of the highest possible standard and responsive to changing teaching demands. * Maintains a strong focus on personal and professional growth including continual professional development, participation in professional learning communities, group reflection and practice. * Has the confidence, resilience and optimism to maintain an emotional balance in challenging situations and the capacity to work through these situations. * Is open and responsive to constructive feedback regarding their professional practice and, if necessary, seeks appropriate support, advice and guidance. * Is aware of the Teaching Council Code of Professional Conduct and Social Media Guidelines and is familiar with its professional requirements. * Practices within the statutory framework and regulations pertaining to education, including child protection and health and safety guidelines. * Upholds professional integrity for example discretion, confidentiality, loyalty and trust, empathy etc in all aspects of their professional role as a teacher. * Is aware of the broader student context and their requirement to model best practice in all aspect of their role and relationships in the school community. Please check your email (including spam folders) for updates. If you have any technical issues when applying for this position, please contact Jobtrain at; https://jobseekersupport.jobtrain.co.uk/support/home where you can log a ticket or call 0044 161 850 2004\. FAQ's are available in the top right menu once you have registered. (Please note Jobtrain hours are from 9am to 5\.30pm, and queries may take up to 24hrs to be answered).

title: Network Cabling Quality Technician
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- AWS Infrastructure Services (AIS) designs, plans, delivers, and operates all AWS global infrastructure. We keep the cloud running by supporting AWS data centers and the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that give customers continual access to innovation. We solve complex problems with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain. You'll join software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other essential roles. You'll collaborate across AWS to deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing capacity at the lowest cost for customers. In this role, you will: Work with Network Deployment and Vendor Management teams to integrate quality assurance into cable installation processes throughout the project lifecycle. You'll participate in reviews of training curriculums and cabling design documents, providing Quality Assurance representation to ensure standards are met during cable installation activities. You'll influence partner teams and cabling vendors to adopt quality assurance checklists that validate conformance to both Amazon and jurisdictional standards during critical milestone checks for cable installation and testing activities. You'll bring the voice of the customer forward by gathering feedback from internal stakeholders and cabling vendors, then refining quality processes based on their needs. You'll verify that cable installations meet standards by reviewing documentation and data from milestone inspections and cable validation testing. You'll drive cable validation testing adoption and defect management while establishing performance benchmarks to improve vendor performance and enhance quality metrics. You'll document and align cable installation standards and standard operating procedures as new technology and cabling methods integrate into our infrastructure. You’ll also conduct training with partner teams and provide guidelines to cabling vendors to ensure consistent execution across all network installations. This role matters because cable installation quality directly impacts data center network reliability and customer experience. You'll own the quality process from participating in cabling design reviews through training delivery, ensuring our infrastructure meets the standards our customers depend on. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities * Support Quality Assurance related programs to drive world\-class quality in network cabling in the new constructions of Data Centers to achieve the best operating, lowest cost infrastructure in the world. The Network Cabling Quality Technician will be obsessed with quality, engineering and design continuous improvements * Partner with Network Deployment teams to adopt defined quality assurance checklists, gather feedback, and generate recommendations to improve checklists. Work with partner teams to track cabling scope changes and perform impact analysis to address business needs * Partner with the Vendor Management team to drive cable vendor quality, provide vendor performance metrics through business reviews, and refine processes to improve vendor performance * Support cable installation benchmark according to Amazon policies and industry standards; maintain cable installation standards, checklists, standard operating procedures and best practices guidelines * Conduct Network Cabling Installation Quality Training Programs, and the creation of training materials, to support internal and external partner and stakeholder teams * Collaborate with stakeholder teams to capture metrics data such as inspection reports and cable test results; track defects for root cause analysis, generate recommendations, evaluate vendor performances and escalate issues to respective owners for remediation plans * Track and report on project milestones, vendor performance metrics, and quality KPIs including First Pass Yield, defect rates, and schedule adherence across multiple concurrent projects * Support continuous improvement initiatives by capturing lessons learned, facilitating post\-project reviews, and implementing process improvements to enhance future project delivery * Identify opportunities to reduce Cost of Poor Quality and improve overall project efficiency * Regional and Local Travel up to 25% to attend site visits and meetings for onsite quality standards review and collect feedback from onsite personnel A day in the life Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a highly innovative company that collaborates across disciplines to produce new technology that changes our world. The AWS Quality Team is a fast\-growing global team that is rethinking the implementation and design for the critical arteries that connect the various pieces of hardware inside AWS’ data centers and edge sites. As a member of this team, you will be charged with visiting our data centers and ensuring that we are following AWS and Industry standards while also collecting lessons learned so we are continually improving. You will work directly with engineers, managers, and operations teams across the company to develop and improve network pathway and installation standards based on these observations and lessons learned. title: Network Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Senior Network Engineer to support and enhance enterprise network infrastructure within a large\-scale operational environment. The successful candidate will play a key role in maintaining secure, resilient, and high\-performing network services. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and support of enterprise firewall platforms including technologies such as Fortinet, Check Point, Palo Alto, or Azure Firewall * Management of firewall policies, NAT, VPN connectivity (site\-to\-site and remote access), and security rulebases * Troubleshooting complex network, routing, and connectivity issues across LAN, WAN, and cloud environments * Support and maintenance of core network services including switching, routing, DNS, DHCP, and network segmentation * Implementation and support of network security controls in collaboration with cybersecurity teams * Participation in infrastructure upgrades, network changes, and implementation projects * Operational support for cloud networking environments, preferably Microsoft Azure * Maintenance of network documentation, configurations, and operational procedures * Knowledge transfer and collaboration with internal technical teams **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5–10\+ years’ experience in Network Engineering or Infrastructure Engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience managing enterprise firewall environments * Excellent troubleshooting and diagnostic capability across complex networks * Experience supporting enterprise LAN/WAN infrastructure * Strong understanding of networking protocols and security principles * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced operational environment **Desirable Experience** * Experience with Azure networking and cloud security services * Exposure to regulated or compliance\-driven environments * Familiarity with security frameworks and governance standards * Experience working within large enterprise IT operations teams **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Operations & Brand Intern, Summer @ Mafia Matcha
description: ***First filter: you already drink matcha. Like, properly. You have a tin at home, you have opinions about it, and you’ve definitely paid €6 for one in town and not regretted it. If that’s not you, this one isn’t for you!*** We’re Mafia Matcha \- an Irish matcha brand now stocked in over 200 coffee shops and retailers across Ireland, plus our own DTC channel and pop\-ups around Dublin. We’re a small team building something a bit louder, a bit more fun, and a lot better\- tasting than what’s already out there \- and we’re growing fast enough that the founder genuinely needs a second pair of hands across ops and brand this summer. This isn’t a “shadow the team and write a report at the end” internship. You’ll be in the warehouse packing real orders, in the office throwing ideas around, and out at pop\-ups talking matcha with strangers \- often on the same day. **What a week actually looks like:** One day you’re at our Sandyford warehouse packing 30 orders, restocking, and making sure the shelves don’t descend into chaos. The next you’re at the office helping us figure out which 15 creators to send PR boxes to, drafting the outreach, and getting the boxes out the door. Saturday you might be at a pop\-up in town whisking matcha for people who’ve never tried it and convincing them they’ve been missing out. Monday you bring back three ideas from what you saw at the pop\-up and we actually try one of them. That’s the job. Ops \+ brand \+ customer\-facing, mixed together, every week different. **The split, roughly** * \~50% Operations \- packing orders, warehouse organisation, stock, the day\-to\-day of a small brand running properly * \~30% Brand \& PR \- running our PR/UGC outreach end\-to\-end: building lists, writing outreach, prepping boxes, following up, learning what works * \~20% Pop\-ups \& events \- repping the brand in person, serving matcha, educating people, being the face of Mafia Matcha out in the wild The split flexes week to week. Some weeks are heavier on events, some on ops. Some weekend availability is needed \- pop\-ups happen when our customers are free, which is often Saturdays. You’ll thrive here if you: * Are a 2nd or 3rd year Business\-oriented student (marketing, commerce, entrepreneurship, management \- anything in that family) * Genuinely drink matcha and can talk about it without faking it * Are obsessed with brands and social \- you follow visual brands, you screenshot good packaging, you have opinions about why some DTC brands fly and others die * Bring ideas without being asked. If you notice the order labels could be laid out better, you say so. If a creator we should DM isn\&\#39;t on our list, you add them. * Are organised enough that nothing slips, and chilled enough to roll with a startup week where the plan changes on Wednesday * Are warm and confident with strangers at pop\-ups \- not performative, just genuinely happy to chat about something you love * Have your own car \- non\-negotiable. You\&\#39;ll be moving between Sandyford and pop\-ups around Dublin with equipment. * Live in South Dublin \- Dundrum, Stillorgan, Sandyford, Foxrock, Blackrock, Dún Laoghaire etc. Max \~20 min by car from Sandyford. **This isn’t the job for you if:** * You don’t drink matcha and you’re hoping you’ll “grow into it” * You don’t drive or don’t have access to a car * You want a structured corporate internship with a defined programme and a manager telling you what to do every hour * The idea of packing orders or doing manual warehouse work feels beneath you (we all do it, including the founders) * You’re not based in or near South Dublin and the commute would be a stretch * “Just doing what’s on your job description” is your default setting **Logistics:** * Pay: €16,5/hour \- paid for every hour worked, including weeks that run longer * Hours: 20/week base, some weeks more with prior agreement * Start: Monday 22nd June 2026 * End: Sunday 14th September 2026, with strong possibility of extension into a part\-time role through the academic year (hours flexed around your timetable). That’s the actual goal here, we’d rather invest in one person who sticks for another year or two than re\-hire every summer. * Location: Sandyford\-based, with pop\-ups around Dublin and beyond * Application deadline: 9th June 2026 **How to apply:** Send the following to jobs@mafiamatcha.com: 1\. Your CV (one page, please) 2\. A short intro \- written, voice note, or Loom, whatever you prefer. Tell us why Mafia Matcha specifically, and your honest matcha story (when, how often, favourite way to drink it). 3\. Pick one: a) Name 3 creators or press contacts you’d want us to send a PR box to, and one line on why each b) Tell us one thing about Mafia Matcha’s social / brand / website you’d change, and what you’d try instead Follow us before you apply: @mafiamatcha IG/TikTok \- it’ll tell you more about who we are than any job ad can:)

title: Operations Administrator
description: **About Us** At BidX1, we're changing the way property is bought and sold. We’re disruptors – BidX1’s digital auction platform connects users across the globe with real estate opportunities in four markets and enables them to complete the entire purchase online using technology developed in Dublin. We’re high\-growth – BidX1 held the first online auctions in Ireland in 2015 and by 2019, we had launched operations in Cyprus, the UK and South Africa. We’ve sold more than 20,000 properties to date, raising more than €4 billion, with bidders from more than 60 countries. We’re sustainable – BidX1 has been carbon neutral since 2019, joined the UN Global Compact in 2021, and submitted our first report to the Carbon Disclosure Project in 2022\. We’re good at what we do – BidX1 has been recognised by Deloitte as a ‘Best Managed Company’, received backing from Enterprise Ireland, and won numerous awards including a KPMG Property Excellence Award, an Irish Times Innovation Award, and an Estates Gazette Tech Award. But that’s enough about us – we’d like to meet you! **The Role** You will work to support the sales team and coordinate auction operations. No property or auction experience is required and training on our systems will be provided. Our office is based in Ballsbridge, Dublin 4, and the role requires working from the office 3 days a week, with the option to work from home 2 days a week. **Responsibilities** * Use of Dynamics 365 CRM system to add properties and other information * Supporting of sales team * Advertising viewings * Requesting of photography and signboards * Issuing of contracts to clients * Testing of system updates and changes * Answering customers calls and email enquiries * Performing Anti\-Money Laundering checks * Approving of bidder registrations * General administrative duties **Qualifications, Skills \& Experience** **Key Skills:** * Clear, polite and professional written and verbal communication. * Strong organisation skills with the ability to manage a busy workload. * A team player – key that you can work collectively as well as independently. * Comfortable taking calls from customers and answering their questions * Thorough understanding of Outlook, Excel, and Word. **Experience:** * Minimum of 2 years in an administrative role * Experience of answering calls from customers * General knowledge of Microsoft Office, mainly Excel \& Outlook **Desirable:** * Microsoft Dynamics 365 experience

title: Optical NDE II, Optical Long Haul Network Deployment
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Do you like to use networking concepts and system development engineering to deliver simple, sustainable and repeatable solutions? Would you like your work to play a key role in driving the stability and sustainability of next\-generation IP networks? Amazon Web Services is looking for an Optical Network Development Engineer to support and deploy one of the world’s largest and most complex telecommunication networks. Our goal is to become “The Infrastructure Platform” to the world and customers demand the highest quality and reliability for their services. As we expand at a tremendous rate it is our responsibility to maintain that quality and reliability. We look for innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand, while driving complex issues to resolution. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities As an Optical Deployment Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will: * Design of optical solutions, hands\-on to support deployment and manage rollout of AWS global DWDM network; * Drive optical scaling tasks including fiber quality reviews and generate cutsheets and configs to meet the demand of AWS customers; * Define and configure management solution to support optical network remote monitoring and device visibility; * Work closely with other AWS Networking teams (field tech and fiber teams) to ensure optical network is scaled in time to support business; * Review and implement changes on optical systems using manual CLI config or automated process via script; * Ensure that optical network deployment tasks adhere to the defined AWS network standards; * Execute implementation tasks following operational excellence standards and propose improvements in AWS DWDM network quality and reliability; * Design and implement scalable operational processes to sustain and expand our optical network infrastructure; * Lead end\-to\-end product evaluation and drive strategic commercial tender processes, ensuring optimal vendor selection and cost efficiency * Troubleshoot complex problems on optical networks and eventually propose innovative solutions to improve the deployment agility; * Lead and execute operational excellence programs that measurably improve DWDM network quality and reliability, reducing service\-impacting events and raising the bar on optical network performance; Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. This is an amazing opportunity in terms of responsibility, interesting challenges and high visibility. A day in the life This role oversees deployment of Metro and Long Haul systems and all processes related to those deliverables. It includes the design of the projects, generation of all technical documents, updating internal tools, deployment of systems in the real network and handover for operations. Also the deployment team is responsible to participate in testing new solutions and define technical requirements. Automation is a very important topic in this team to improve the deployment process, reduce the manual activities and make interaction between internal tools. About the team Internet Edge provides high\-performing Internet connectivity for a wide range of use cases, such as retail (amazon.com), video streaming (Prime Video), content delivery (CloudFront), DNS (Route53\), AWS services (such as EC2/S3\), consumer devices (such as FireTV/Kindle/Echo), and more. Within AWS Networking, the Internet Edge organization is responsible for connecting Amazon to the global Internet. With connections to thousands of unique external networks in hundreds of locations across the world, our mission is to deliver an Internet connectivity experience that sets the global standard for performance, availability, security, and cost, making Amazon the clear choice for our customers’ needs. The Long Haul Deployment team is responsible for the installation and commissioning of the DWDM systems for Optical Backbone. Our mission is to deliver unconstrained inter\-regional transport at the lowest cost, with the most available product conceivable to AWS customers, making it invisible. The team vends capacity to internet backbone customers that provides low latency, high availability and supports features our customers expect. About AWS Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Information Technology, a related technical field or equivalent experience * 4\+ years' experience with major DWDM platforms; * 4\+ years' experience with optical planning, modelling tools and hands\-on implementation; * Experience with the rollout of large DWDM networks in Metro and Long Haul spaces; **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Knowledge of networking fundamentals * Knowledge of network design, protocols and Layer 1/2 troubleshooting * Experience with optical planning and modelling tools; and the rollout of large DWDM networks. * Optical network design, planning and implementation skills; Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. title: Part-TPart-Time Painter / Handyman Assistantime Handyman / Painter Assistant
description: **About Us** Night Owls Decorators is a busy family\-run business working across over 300 managed properties in Dublin. We are looking for a **practical, reliable, and detail\-focused** person to join our team for part\-time morning work. This is **not a general cleaning role**. We are specifically looking for someone who is comfortable with **painting and basic handyman tasks** and who can work **cleanly, quickly, and with care** in occupied and vacant apartments. **The Role** The work typically includes: * interior wall painting * cutting\-in and touch\-up painting * filling, sanding, and basic wall preparation * silicone sealing in bathrooms and kitchens * replacing small fittings such as switches, handles, door stops, etc. * basic handyman tasks * lawn mowing and basic outdoor tidy\-up * keeping tools, machines, and work areas clean and organised **What We Need** We are **not** looking for someone who only wants light work or who has no experience using tools. We are looking for someone who: * has **real hands\-on experience** with painting or handyman work * is comfortable using **basic tools and equipment** * works **fast but neatly** * understands the importance of **clean finishes and tidy work** * takes care of tools and reports any damaged equipment immediately * can follow instructions properly without constant supervision * is punctual and dependable **Hours** * **Part\-time** * Usually **08:00am – 12:00noon** * Up to **20 hours per week** * Some weeks may be quieter than others, depending on workload **Location** We work at different sites across Dublin, including: * City Centre * South Dublin * Dundrum * Lucan * Cherrywood * and other areas. You must be able to get to site **on time every morning**. **Important** This role suits someone who genuinely likes practical work and wants to be useful in a small team. It is **not suitable** for someone who: * dislikes physical work * works slowly * leaves mess behind * is not confident with tools * needs constant reminders about basic standards **Requirements** * Experience in **interior painting, silicone sealing, or handyman work** * Basic spoken **English** * Valid right to work in Ireland * Reliable and able to travel to sites across Dublin * **Driving licence preferred** * Previous experience in maintenance, decorating, property services, or similar work is a strong advantage **Please answer all screening questions clearly. Applications with missing or vague answers may not be considered.** Pay: From €16\.00 per hour Application question(s): * Which of the following have you done before as part of paid work? * interior painting * cutting\-in * filling / sanding * silicone sealing * replacing switches / sockets * small repairs / handyman tasks * lawn mowing / garden tidy\-up * Are you comfortable using tools and working independently once shown the task? * This role is part\-time, usually 08:00–12:00, up to 20 hours per week, depending on workload. Are you fully flexible with this? * Are you able to travel to different job sites across Dublin and arrive on time in the morning? * Please briefly describe the painting or handyman jobs you have done most recently. Experience: * Handyman: 2 years (required) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: People Consultant
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** One of the key drivers to our success is an exceptional emphasis on our people. We believe that our competitive advantage lies in our talent and that we can build a sustainable advantage by enabling individuals and teams to contribute their full potential. The People Consultant team helps to drive those efforts across our Tech, Core, and Business Organizations. This includes supporting our leaders and managers through scale, creating an environment where Stripes of all backgrounds can thrive, aligning our business strategy with our people strategy, using data to develop best practices from first principles. We are committed to building an inclusive work environment where all Stripes feel welcomed as their authentic selves—inclusive of all genders, sexual orientations, ethnicities, races, education, ages, and other personal characteristics. We work on broadening our internal diversity because we want to be culturally equipped to build products and solutions for our diverse user base. What you’ll do As a People Consultant, you’ll help make Stripe a great place to work and grow. You’ll work with business leaders, managers, and employees across Stripe to strategize and implement systems, processes, and programs to help people succeed. **Responsibilities** * Partner with and advise front line managers on performance management, coaching, and employee relations. Collaborate closely with Employee Relations and Legal teams to foster sound business decisions. * Work with the Legal team and external consultants to help ensure Stripe’s compliance with local labor regulations * Advise and consult front line management on Stripe People policies and best practices, in collaboration with fellow People team members and counsel as necessary * Partner directly with People Partners (HRBPs), managers, and leaders on wider organizational strategy and initiatives, including reorganizations * Facilitate smooth roll out of key People programs, including front line manager support for performance review cycles, compensation cycles, employee engagement surveys and ad hoc programs * Analyze and present on organizational health trends and insights to managers and leaders; manage tracking and reporting of some key people data to inform team strategy * Ensure excellent employee experience through key life cycle stages including the departure processes Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Minimum of 3\+ years of HR experience; at least 1 year in a client facing HR role (e.g. HR Business Partner, Employee Relations partner) * Proven track record of partnering directly with managers on performance management, coaching, and resolving sensitive employee relations issues * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills demonstrated through success diagnosing problems using data, reasoning clearly and determining viable solutions, and remaining flexible in those solutions’ implementation * Experience utilizing company policies and practices to determine appropriate recommendations for action * Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience working in a fast paced, growing environment, preferably in a technology company or a large scale organization * Project management skills, with an ability to design and execute clear processes while successfully engaging peers and stakeholders to deliver work on schedule * Proven ability to identify areas for improvement and escalate recurring themes to key decision makers when needed In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €88,000 \- €132,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Platform Architect
description: **Platform Architect** **Fulltime \- Permanent position** **Hybrid 2 days onsite based in Dublin city center.** Our High Profile Client seeks a Platform Architect to lead the design, development, and management of its Cloud Azure platform and DevOps. This hands\-on role requires deep expertise in Azure or AWS, DevOps, and cloud\-native technologies, supporting client’s digital transformation and growth strategy **Key Responsibilities** * Own and drive the roadmap, capabilities, and governance of client’s Azure\-based DevOps and Cloud Platform. * Analyse, design, and optimise DevOps and cloud engineering practices across teams. * Implement automation solutions using Azure DevOps, Jenkins, Terraform, and Azure\-native services. * Champion best practices in software development, cloud operations, IaC, CI/CD, observability, cost management, and security. * Establish and maintain CI/CD environments to accelerate delivery and platform evolution. * Provide expert guidance to development, platform, and operations teams throughout the project lifecycle. * Monitor and manage technical operations and platform reliability using Azure\-native tools (e.g., Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Defender for Cloud). * Providing expertise to ensure solutions are scalable, reliable, secure, and compliant, aligned with enterprise architecture. * Develop reusable patterns, reference architectures, and automation frameworks to standardise platform usage. **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience in systems/solutions architecture, with strong exposure to cloud\-native and hybrid enterprise environments. * Deep architectural knowledge of Azure Cloud Platform, including core services, PaaS, Azure DevOps, Landing Zones, and governance. * Strong knowledge of CI/CD pipelines (preferably Azure DevOps; Jenkins also valuable). * Hands\-on experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) for Azure resource deployment. * Experience with artefact management, release engineering, and automated software delivery. * Good knowledge of cloud networking (VNETs, private endpoints, load balancers, DNS, firewalls, hybrid connectivity). * Systems integration experience across multi\-vendor and multi\-cloud platforms. * Working knowledge of at least one API Gateway (Azure API Management preferred). * Experience with container platforms (Kubernetes, AKS, Docker). * Familiarity with Atlassian toolset (JIRA, Confluence) * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, able to explain complex concepts to diverse audiences. * Experience mentoring technical staff and guiding teams through cloud transformation. * Strong problem\-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple workstreams. * Fluent in English (written and verbal). **Desired Skills \& Experience** * Experience with container orchestration (Kubernetes/AKS, Docker Swarm). * Experience with Database DevOps/DB CI/CD tooling (e.g., Liquibase). * Knowledge of the insurance or financial services industry. * Working knowledge of German an asset. * Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related discipline.

title: Porter
description: **This job is with Compass Group UK \& Ireland, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ\+ business community. title: Portfolio Management Compliance Analyst
description: Overview: **The Opportunity:** The Portfolio Management Compliance (“PMC”) Analyst (“Analyst”) is responsible for helping to perform Portfolio Management Compliance team tasks, requirements, and protocols within the Fisher Group of companies. The PMC Analyst works cross\-functionally with the members of the Fisher Group Legal, Communications, and Compliance Department (“LCCD”) to help Fisher entities satisfy their portfolio management regulatory requirements. The Analyst is a member of the Portfolio Management Compliance team, who provide oversight and challenge of the Fisher Group fulfilment of trading\-related requirements. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Monitors compliance of the Fisher trading function business to completely and efficiently meet regulatory requirements, particularly in relation to regulatory reporting * Ensure all required trade reporting to local regulators of the Fisher Group is being met in a timely and accurate manner, according to MiFIR and EMIR guidelines, elevating to a senior member of the team if issues arise * Monitor regulatory changes which affect the business and perform gap analysis on those regulations * Monitors internal trade process adherence reporting, elevating flags as needed * Works closely with the CGA team (Client Guidelines and Assurance), as appropriate * Responsible for quarterly and annual funds (US, Australian, Irish, Canadian) investment fund manager attestations and coordinating with Finance on regulatory reporting * Coordinates oversight for Commodity Pool Operators (CPOs) and Commodity Trading Advisors (CTA) exemptions and adherence * Responsible for procedure review and updates * Maintain a formal process for International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA) compliance, in addition to the Options Clearing Corporation (OCC IDs), Depository Trust \& Clearing Corporation (DTCC) and other market facing portals * Contributes towards IMAs and RFPs * Support projects related to regulatory reporting * Works onsite, under direct supervision, in a team\-based and open office environment **Your Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required * 2\+ years of experience in investment management regulatory compliance preferred * Working knowledge of both US and European regulatory framework, particularly USPR, EMIR, and MiFID II preferred * Proven experience of trade execution within a high\-volume trading environment, delivering consistent performance under pressure * Superior attention to detail and excellent organisational skills * Excellent oral and written communication skills * Experience at working both independently and in a team\-oriented, collaborative environment * Ability to work independently and conform to shifting priorities, demands and timelines through analytical and problem\-solving capabilities * Working knowledge of MS Office * Able to interface with different departments within the company **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * *Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency.* FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Product Cybersecurity Engineer
description: **Company Profile** Hanley Energy is a globally recognised award\-winning innovator in energy management, critical power and data centre control solutions for a diverse range of business sectors. Headquartered in Stamullen, Co. Meath Hanley Energy also have additional facilities in Kingscourt, Co. Cavan and Dundalk, Co. Louth. With offices in the US, Sweden, Australia, South Africa and Germany, we have a wealth of experience and expertise in supplying, designing, installing and supporting energy monitoring and management systems, critical power and data centre control solutions for a range of Irish and international clients in a variety of business sectors. **Role Description:** Cybersecurity is now a core requirement for any product placed on the market, driven by customer expectations, evolving international standards, and emerging regulatory frameworks across our key regions. We are creating a new Product Cybersecurity Engineer role within our Technology and Cybersecurity department to support the day\-to\-day product security operations that keep our hardware, firmware, and software secure across their lifecycle based in our Stamullen Head office on a hybrid working model ( 3 days on site) This is a development role: we are looking for someone with a methodical, process\-driven mindset and a clear focus on cybersecurity, who wants to build deep expertise in product security with our mentoring behind them. **Role Responsibilities:** Working alongside engineering leadership, R\&D, firmware, software, quality, and supply\-chain teams, you will: * Monitor vulnerabilities affecting our products and components using our internal product traceability platform, triage findings, and coordinate remediation with the relevant engineering teams. * Manage Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs): request, validate, and store SBOMs from our suppliers; generate and maintain SBOMs for software and components we develop in\-house; ensure SBOMs stay current as products evolve. * Help maintain product security procedures, including software version control and traceability, patch management, secure update processes, and unique\-credential and secrets\-handling practices across product lines. * Coordinate supplier security communications: request and review supplier security information, follow up on missing artefacts, track supplier responses to vulnerabilities, and maintain the documentation that supports our supply\-chain assurance. * Support external communications related to product security, including liaising with customers on advisories and questionnaires, communicating with relevant EU and national cybersecurity bodies, and helping to coordinate incoming product security enquiries. * Maintain technical documentation supporting product security claims, customer assurances, and internal audits. * Support coordinated vulnerability disclosure processes as they mature. Your responsibilities will grow as you do, with scope to specialise based on your interests and strengths. **Role Requirements:** Essentials: * A degree in Computer Science, Electronic or Electrical Engineering, Cybersecurity, Information Systems, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. * Around 2\+ years in a technical role such as IT security, IT operations, software or firmware development, network engineering, embedded systems, QA or test, supplier or compliance management, or a similar role in a regulated environment. * A track record of engagement with cybersecurity (through work, study, certifications, or technical projects) and a clear ambition to specialise in product security. * Strong attention to detail and a structured, methodical approach to documentation, tracking, and follow\-up. * Comfortable reading and reasoning about technical artefacts such as SBOMs, version manifests, release notes, and vulnerability advisories. * Excellent written and verbal communication in English. This role involves frequent communication with suppliers, customers, and external bodies. Desirable: * Awareness of cybersecurity frameworks and standards such as IEC 62443, ETSI EN 303 645, ISO/IEC 27001, or NIST CSF. * Exposure to vulnerability management workflows, SBOM tooling (CycloneDX, SPDX), or vulnerability data sources (NVD, OSV, CISA KEV). * Experience working with suppliers on security or quality artefacts, or with customers on security questionnaires and audits. * Background in or exposure to energy, utilities, data centres, or industrial automation. * Familiarity with OT protocols such as Modbus, BACnet, MQTT, or OPC UA. * Any security certifications, including entry\-level ones (CompTIA Security\+, ISC2 CC, ISA/IEC 62443 Fundamentals). **Company Benefits:** * Salary with excellent career development opportunities * Company Healthcare Plan * Company Pension Scheme * Death in Service Life Insurance * Employee Assistance Programme \& Wellbeing Programme * Academic assistance \& Structured In\-house training * Hanley Energy Affinity Scheme * Free parking at all locations * Hybrid Work Model where applicable
